commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real CLI proof plus maintainer selection among competing same-issue PRs; there is no narrow automated code repair remaining on this branch.; Cleared: The diff only changes memory-core runtime/test files and does not touch dependencies, CI, package metadata, secrets, permissions, or supply-chain execution paths. (sha=2212ea0516bdf34d56082858059f10bb5d3cbd2b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes status-purpose memory manager creation await the existing session startup dirty-file scan and adds a regression test for unindexed session transcripts.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +22. Total +35 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main constructs plain status managers as transient, skips session startup catch-up, and reports dirty from manager flags rather than comparing session files with indexed sessions rows.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied real CLI proof plus maintainer selection among competing same-issue PRs; there is no narrow automated code repair remaining on this branch.

target.title: fix(memory): detect unindexed session transcripts in status mode (fixes #97814)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97814.

`openclaw memory status` reports `dirty=false` when unindexed session transcripts exist on disk. This is a **false-clean state**: the operator sees no backlog, but memory search silently misses the unindexed transcript.

**Root cause:** The status-purpose `MemoryIndexManager` init skips `ensureSessionStartupCatchup()`, which is the only path that detects on-disk session files without corresponding `memory_index_sources` rows and marks `sessionsDirty=true`.

## Changes Made

Move the status-mode dirty detection from the constructor (fire-and-forget via `void`) into the `create()` factory method where it is **awaited** before returning the manager. This guarantees `sessionsDirty` is set before any caller reads `manager.status()`.

- Runs `markSessionStartupCatchupDirtyFiles()` in status mode — checks for on-disk session files without corresponding `memory_index_sources` rows
- Does **not** trigger a full `sync()` — status remains lightweight
- The full catchup+sync runs on the next non-transient manager cycle (CLI sync or normal runtime init)

## Evidence

**Unit tests (22/22 passed):**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-status-state.test.ts
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.startup-catchup.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  21 passed (21)

# New regression test for status-purpose flow:
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts -t status-purpose
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  1 passed (1)
```

**New regression test — `purpose: "status"` manager flow:**
```typescript
it("status-purpose manager detects unindexed session transcripts as dirty", async () => {
  const sessionDir = resolveSessionTranscriptsDirForAgent("main");
  await fs.writeFile(path.join(sessionDir, "status-dirty-test.jsonl"), ...);

  const manager = await getFreshManager(cfg, "status");  // purpose: "status"
  // The fix ensures markSessionStartupCatchupDirtyFiles() runs
  // during create() — awaited, not fire-and-forget.

  const result = manager.status();
  expect(result.dirty).toBe(true);  // ✅ unindexed transcript detected
});
```

**Real detection flow:**
```
=== Step 1: Files on disk ===
  indexed-tr.jsonl (16 bytes)
  unindexed-tr.jsonl (16 bytes)

=== Step 2: memory_index_sources rows ===
  sessions/main/indexed-tr.jsonl  ✓ indexed
  (unindexed-tr.jsonl → no row found)

=== Step 3: markSessionStartupCatchupDirtyFiles() ===
  → MISSING: sessions/main/unindexed-tr.jsonl has no index entry
    → ADDED to sessionsDirtyFiles | sessionsDirty = true

=== Step 4: Result ===
  sessionsDirtyFiles: ["sessions/main/unindexed-tr.jsonl"]
  sessionsDirty: true
  Sync triggered: false
```

After the fix, detection runs **before** `manager.status()` is called, so the dirty flag is always accurate when read.

## Review

- Manually verified: code inspection + regression test confirms detection completes before `status()` reads the dirty flag
- AI-assisted: Yes
target.author: zw-xysk
